title: For Immediate Release

raw_text:
```json
{
  "distinct_claims": [
    "Tropical forests cover roughly seven percent of the Earth’s surface but harbor close to half of all species on Earth.",
    "An estimated 18 million acres of forest are lost every year, largely due to the expansion of agribusiness plantations.",
    "Tropical deforestation and related land-use changes are responsible for nearly a quarter of global carbon dioxide emissions.",
    "Tropical deforestation is a major contributor to the global biodiversity crisis.",
    "California state contracts involving commodities that put tropical forests at risk would require contractors to maintain a 'forest policy'.",
    "Hundreds of consumer brands, commodity traders, and national and subnational governments support ending deforestation and primary forest degradation.",
    "Many brands and governments are committed to zero deforestation in their supply chains.",
    "A number of nation-states, notably Norway and France, have adopted 'deforestation-free' procurement approaches.",
    "The climate emergency is driven by the addiction to animal agriculture and fossil fuels.",
    "California’s Deforestation-Free Procurement Act aims to stem the crisis of deforestation at its root.",
    "The destruction of tropical forests is linked to the demand for cheaper commodities."
  ]
}
```
